description:
I'm Tom, and this is the tale of ticking off one of my bucket-list boxes: buggering about on a boat with my son Otto.
This is the story of our adventures aboard our new (to us) yacht, ROLI in the English Channel. The eventual dream? Drying out on the Ganilly sand bar in the Scillies for a game of cricket — nicked from a YouTuber called Harry Dwyer, who made it look far too tempting.
That's a few years off yet. In the meantime, I hope you enjoy our tales and travails en route.

title: Moore than talk...
author: Diana Moore
description:
Moore Than Talk!  The podcast for anyone working to make good things happen with their community.

For many years I’ve helped people set up, run or grow a community enterprise to get things done for, and with, their community. There is so much to learn by diving deep into the stories of those who’ve already taken action.

Some are inspired by a determination for a better neighbourhood, or to respond to an unmet need or great idea, some want to stop a threat to a loved asset or local environment, others want to bring their community together

I’ll be talking to local leaders and activists about how their community enterprise works and the difference it makes. I’ll also be sharing top tips from those who provide technical support and help for community enterprises.

I hope their experiences, insights, learning how to  navigating the inevitable bureaucracy, overcoming challenges and seizing opportunities will offer practical help, so you can make good things happen with your community.

Thanks to Will Davis for production.

title: ACC Football Addiction (Atlantic Coast Conference)
author: College Football Addiction
description:
From Doak Campbell to Death Valley, from Miami to Tobacco Road, from the transfer portal to the 12-team playoff — we are talking all things ACC Football here on ACC Football Addiction.

We cover the championships, the coastal chaos, NIL news, and the coast-to-coast passion that defines the conference.

Subscribe on YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@accfootballaddiction
Follow us on Twitter: https://twitter.com/TJ_Pittinger

title: Golden Archives Podcast
author: John California
description:
Welcome to Golden Archives, a nostalgia podcast exploring the history, memories, and cultural impact of McDonald’s. From the McDonald brothers and Ray Kroc to classic menu items, Happy Meal toys, commercials, promotions, characters, collectibles, and forgotten favorites, each episode takes a walk down memory lane through the stories behind the Golden Arches.
Independent podcast. Not affiliated with or endorsed by McDonald’s Corporation.

title: The Capital Steward Podcast
author: The Capital Stewards
description:
The Capital Stewards emerges from a three-year research collaboration at The Kirby Laing Centre for Public Theology in Cambridge, England. Each month, our group has met to wrestle with the questions where Christian worldview, capital, and stewardship collide — and now we’re inviting you into the conversation. See more at capitalstewards.co.

Brandon Giella of Snapmarket.co is joined by Erik Averill of AWM, The Athlete Family Office, Ben Nicka of Oxford University, and Matt Galyon, an independent consultant, among others. The group is advised by theologian Craig Bartholomew.

This is not another faith-and-finance show. It’s a working conversation among practitioners and scholars who have spent years asking what it actually means to steward capital under God’s economy — and where the dominant assumptions of optimization, return, and productivity break down against the biblical story.

Every episode begins with a single question. We think out loud, draw on Scripture, and invite you in.

Each episode, Harsh, Lead News Editor at Invezz, sits down with analysts and investors to break down one big idea shaping global markets — from geopolitics and earnings to IPOs and everything in between. No noise, no filler. Just sharp analysis on the trades, the trends, and the people moving money. Because in markets, for every winner there's a loser. This is Zero Sum.

We’re cutting through the corporate noise with the precision of a laser show to bring you the stocks, trends, and headline news you actually give a damn about. From The Inferno (what’s hot right now) to The Slow Burn (deep-dive tactics), we deliver high-ROI strategies with a side of bass.
Strap in for case studies that actually convert and a daily IQ test to see if you’re as sharp as you think you are. No fluff, no suits, just pure marketing euphoria. Stay lit. Stay profitable.

title: When Hosting a Bundle Makes Sense (and When it Doesn't) with Val Selby
author: Nicole Batey
description: Hosting a bundle sounds glamorous, right? Authority. List growth. Big numbers. Main character energy.  But before you grab the mic and declare yourself the host, Val Selby is here with the real talk.  In this track, Val breaks down when hosting is actually the smart move — and when it’s just another shiny object. She walks through the capacity question, the audience question, and the strategy question most people skip.  Because hosting isn’t just about gathering contributors and sending emails. It’s about intention, positioning, and knowing what you want the bundle to lead to.  If you’ve been thinking, “Should I host one?” This is your clarity moment before you press go. 💿✨

Hosted by Brandon O., this horror movie podcast blends laugh-out-loud commentary with genuine film analysis — covering everything from classic slashers to modern found footage and indie horror.
Each season explores its own theme with a different horror film recapped every episode. Whether you're a lifelong horror lover or trying something new, Terrorific delivers the perfect mix of insight and humor. Tap in every other Tuesday for all-new episodes, and remember – always keep it spooky. Subscribe on Spotify, Apple Podcasts, or wherever you listen to podcasts.

title: Rights & Realities: Remembering 1986
author: Te Kāhui Tika Tangata Human Rights Commission
description:
Forty years ago, on 9 July 1986, the Homosexual Law Reform Act ended the threat of prosecution for gay men and opened the door for progress - a watershed in Aotearoa New Zealand's human rights history. 

Rights & Realities: Remembering 1986 is a new five-part series that looks at the events leading up to the passing of Labour MP Fran Wilde’s Bill, and beyond, through the voices of activists, politicians, scholars, and the community leaders who lived it. It celebrates how far we've come and acknowledges the Rainbow communities who don’t yet enjoy the advantages that generation fought to achieve.

The series features archival audio and conversations with Fran Wilde, Professor Elizabeth Kerekere, Dr Stephen Rainbow, Phylesha Brown-Acton, Louisa Wall, Professor Chris Brickell, Linda Evans, Gavin Young, Prudence Walker and Vinod Bal.

Brought to you by Te Kāhui Tika Tangata Human Rights Commission. For more information on human rights in Aotearoa visit www.tikatangata.org.nz

This is Christopher stepping away from the mic for a minute, sitting down, and talking honestly about faith, kindness, chaplaincy, the road, and the sacred moments hiding in ordinary life. No schedule. No agenda. No invoice. Just honest conversation over a second cup of coffee. New episodes pour when the words won't wait. Tips appreciated. Never required. description:
Join young Emma on her magical journey as she discovers a hidden world where imagination takes flight in *Story Time Tales*. This enchanting collection of short stories, crafted by Laura Rountree Smith, brings to life the whimsical adventures that unfold when children dare to dream. Each tale introduces a vibrant cast of characters, from mischievous fairies to brave young heroes, all facing challenges that spark curiosity and ignite the spirit of adventure.

As Emma navigates through enchanted forests and meets talking animals, she learns invaluable lessons about friendship, courage, and the power of believing in oneself. Listeners will find themselves captivated by stories that blend humor and heart, creating a tapestry of experiences that resonate with young minds and remind adults of the joy of childhood imagination.

What secrets lie in the pages of an old book? How can a simple act of kindness change the course of a story? Each narrative invites listeners to ponder these questions while keeping them on the edge of their seats, eager to discover what happens next. Perfect for bedtime or family listening, *Story Time Tales* is more than just a collection of stories; it's an invitation to explore the boundless possibilities of creativity. The Living Proof Podcast with Larry C., Raw stories of addiction. This project isn't just a show for me. A lot of people who know me, know that this has been my life. For 32 years, I was an overly active alcoholic and addict. Today, I am in recovery. On April 30th, 2026, I reached a mile stone of one year sober for the first time in 32 years.

description:
Life is loud. The noise never stops. And most of us are carrying more than anyone around us knows.

Calm the Chaos is where Wall Street Journal bestselling author and founder of 51 businesses Rich Christiansen sits down with thought leaders, healers, founders, and everyday people who have looked their demons in the eyes and made it out the other side.

These are not surface-level conversations. Each episode goes deep into how sticky the hard things really are, the patterns that keep us stuck, the wounds we carry longer than we should, the chaos that quietly runs our lives, and more importantly, how to get through them. Real stories. Real tools. Real frameworks you can use, drawn from Rich's decades of work with leaders, families, and high performers.

Whether you are pushing through something heavy right now or simply want to live with more clarity and intention, this podcast was built to be a resource for you.

New episodes weekly.

Calm the chaos. Get clear. Go forward.

Perfect for cozy evenings or family gatherings, these short works will resonate with anyone who seeks to celebrate the true meaning of Thanksgiving.

description: The Gateshead Heat Exchange podcast explores how Gateshead is harnessing heat from abandoned coal mines to provide low-carbon heating for local homes. Hosted by Northumbria University researcher Faye Doughty, this three-part series takes listeners behind the scenes of one of the UK's pioneering mine water heat networks. Through conversations with experts, contractors and researchers, the podcast examines how the technology works, what residents can expect during installation, and why community engagement is central to a successful energy transition.

title: The Backup Plan
author: Mél Hogan & co.
description:
The Backup Plan is a podcast series that traces an emerging technology that promises to store infinitely more data at minimal environmental cost. It’s a technology as mysterious and miraculous as it is mundane; as molecular as it is massive. That technology is DNA.


This podcast features conversations with scientists, scholars, industry specialists, archivists, and artists that explore the past, present, and future of data storage, its environmental impacts, and the science behind DNA-based data storage. We hear from companies that are on the forefront of developing this new technology and how they envisage the future of data infrastructures from storage to computing, and archivists who may use DNA-based data storage in the future. We also chat with an artist who creates alternative data imaginaries to critique the political, affective, and environmental dimensions of big tech.


This project is part of Genomic Media, a research project funded by a Social Science and Humanities Research Council Insight Grant (2021-2026), led by Dr. Mél Hogan (PI) and Deb Verhoeven (co-I), in collaboration with scholars, scientists, industry specialists, archivists, and artists. We explore, explain, and expose DNA-based data storage by tracking and documenting the legacies of genetics/genomics sciences and their various applications, in parallel with the advent of computation, internet infrastructure, binary code and their discourses.


Social Science and Humanities Research Council - Insight Grant “Genomic Media: Tracking Sociotechnical Imaginaries of DNA Data Storage”


Full transcripts available here (PDF).

description:
Cold Grind Chronicles is an audio-first mental conditioning protocol for rising competitive football athletes who demand tactical dominance and uncompromising accountability.
Each episode delivers visceral, position-specific mental blueprints designed to install psychological resilience, biomechanical precision, and ice-cold decision-making under pressure. We strip away the noise and fluff to build the raw mental architecture required to dominate on the pitch and operate with discipline in life.

title: Pivoting Towards Retirement
author: Spencer Grosvenor
description:
Show Description:
Hosted by Greg Lynch & Amy Sharpe

Are you ready to take charge of your financial future and build the retirement you've always dreamed of? “Pivoting Towards Retirement” is your go-to podcast for practical guidance, expert insights, and honest conversations about everything retirement-related. Hosts Greg Lynch and Amy Sharpe—seasoned financial professionals with a passion for empowering others—bring you strategies, stories, and actionable tips to help you navigate the evolving landscape of retirement planning.

Whether you’re just starting to consider your retirement options or are in the final stretch, Greg and Amy break down complex topics like investment strategies, healthcare, taxes, lifestyle planning, and more. Tune in each week and pivot towards a more confident, fulfilling retirement. It’s never too early—or too late—to begin your journey!

title: To Know Ends: Honest conversations about aging, illness, and dying well
author: Chesapeake Health Partners
description:
What does it mean to live well — all the way to the end? To Know Ends is a podcast about the conversations we avoid, the care we don't understand, and the people who show up anyway. Each episode features voices from inside and outside the world of hospice and palliative care — nurses, social workers, caregivers, artists, and people who have sat beside someone they loved as they died — speaking honestly about what aging, decline, and dying actually look like, and what becomes possible when we stop running from those realities.
The title means two things at once. To know ends is to understand what a closing looks like. It is also to know no ends — to discover that confronting mortality honestly doesn't shrink a life. It expands it.

title: Conversations With Cashmere
author: Jay Cashmere
description: Conversations with Cashmere is a podcast that highlights people making a difference throughout South Florida and Palm Beach County. Through unfiltered genuine conversations with community leaders, athletes, influencers and entrepreneurs we share the stories behind their impact, purpose and passion for giving back. After my years of telling stories on television. I’m bringing those meaningful conversations directly to you in collaboration with Digital Vibez.

title: Black Stage Matters Podcast
author: Josiah Ray McCruiston
description: Black Stage Matters Podcast is a sacred and strategic conversation about Black theatre, Black performance, Black storytelling, and the cultural power of the stage. Rooted in the ancestral principles of Utu, Ifá, Hekima, and Itan, this podcast explores how Black artists, educators, directors, playwrights, performers, and cultural workers use the stage as a place of truth-telling, healing, resistance, memory, and imagination.

title: Are You Enjoying the Film?
author: Chad and Chase Durham
description: Chad and Chase Durham review and discuss movies and TV shows and even music. They rank things, respond to things, and look at the pop culture landscape.

title: EMDR2U
author: James
description:
EMDR2U uses the principles of EMDR and Bilateral Stimulation to give you transformative audio experiences.

EMDR2U is not designed to replace traditional therapy. However, many people who use EMDR2U regularly notice that they can create positive change in their lives much faster than the old, traditional ways. 

The intro session explains how it all works. 

The two most important sessions are; The 3-minute Reset and Emotional Detox

But there is sooo much more to explore and enjoy.



This podcast uses some of my voice and some AI-generated voices. It is for educational and self-development purposes only and is not a substitute for professional therapy.

title: Code and Cobblestones
author: codeandcobblestones
description:
Code & Cobblestones tells the real stories behind Greater Boston’s founders, funders, and builders. Each episode digs into how local startups got started, what almost killed them, and what’s happening across the city’s tech scene now.
Code and Cobblestones is brought to you by MGMT Boston and Step Function VC, and produced by Obey Creative, a boutique branding and authority media consultancy in Boston. https://obeycreative.com

title: Hawk Talk: Stories from a Destination District-Hillsboro R-3 School District
author: Hillsboro R-3 Communications
description:
Welcome to Hawk Talk, the official podcast of the Hillsboro R-3 School District. As a Destination District, we are committed to keeping our families, staff, students, and community connected to the people, programs, and opportunities that make Hillsboro a great place to learn and grow.
Through conversations with students, teachers, administrators, and community partners, we'll share the stories behind student success, highlight what's happening in our classrooms, and provide insight into the decisions shaping our district's future.

title: Royal S Productions (The Podcast)
author: Sienna Sovereign
description: Royal S Productions (The Podcast) showcases the people, stories, and traditions that shape the western horse industry. In partnership with Dust In The Media, Sienna Sovereign brings you interviews, documentaries, and educational content featuring Ontario's leading horse professionals. From barrel racing, rodeo, breeding, and horse care to mindset, mindfulness, and manifestation, our goal is to educate, inspire, and preserve western culture for riders and horse lovers of every level.

title: Abide and Thrive Podcast
author: Drew and Kelsea
description: Abide and Thrive is a podcast that shares encouragement on relationships. Drew and Kelsea invite you to walk with them as they follow Jesus together. Thriving in anything, relationships or not, is found by abiding in Christ. Tune in weekly to find hope and peace in singleness, dating, marriage, and more!

title: Reasons and Revelations
author: GHAFES Scripture Engagement
description: Conversations around learning, loving, and living scripture. In this podcast, we will dive into multiple topics, both challenging and riveting, to help listeners understand and enjoy engaging scripture.

title: Jax History Podcast
author: Ethan Gregory
description: Uncover the real history of Northeast Florida with deep dives into the stories that shaped Jacksonville and its surrounding areas. Moving far beyond the basic tourist timelines, this podcast delivers rigorous historical analysis and meticulously researched facts. From the pre-colonial era and the clash of empires to the Great Fire of 1901 and modern consolidation, we break down the political, economic, and cultural forces that built the River City. If you want a thorough, unvarnished look at the complex legacy of the First Coast, subscribe for a masterclass in local history.

title: Coweta County Bicentennial Story Booth - Our Community, Our Story
author: Catherine Wickey
description:
Welcome to the Coweta County Story Booth Podcast, Our Community, Our Story.  

In each episode, family members and/or friends will interview one another, share memories, local traditions and experiences growing up in Coweta County.  

These stories are being preserved as part of a living archive for future generations. We hope you enjoy. 


If you’d like to share your own story and help preserve a piece of Coweta County’s history, email us at info@coweta.ga.us.

title: Rebel soul talk
author: Soula and Akashka
description:
Welcome to honest multidimensional conversations over a cup of tea. No masks, no performance—just two friends speaking openly about spirituality, awakening, and what truly matters.

As awakened starseeds and sacred colleagues, we explore the wisdom we receive, challenge limiting beliefs, and speak up about the injustices that disconnect people from their gifts and authentic path. Through our different cultural and spiritual backgrounds, we share a common commitment: honoring the sacred with truth, discernment, love, and courage.

Join us as we dive into topics such as true love, spiritual discernment, narcissism through a spiritual lens, creativity, and the starseed journey. Expect heartfelt conversations, laughter, healing, and a little cosmic magic. Grab a cup of tea, relax, and join us.

With love,

Soula & Akashka


Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Restaurant Leadership Podcast: The Show for Multi-Unit Operators Ready to Scale
author: Christin Marvin
description: Welcome to the Restaurant Leadership Podcast...the show for independent restaurant owners who are done surviving and ready to scale.Your host, Christin Marvin, founder of Columbine Hospitality and creator of the Independent Restaurant Framework (IRF), has spent over two decades in the industry...from line cook at 15 to managing partner by 30. She knows what it takes to build a restaurant group that runs without you in it.Each week, Christin brings you real conversations and straight talk on the three things that actually move the needle in a multi-unit restaurant group: People, Process, and Profit.This podcast is for you if you're ready to stop putting out fires and start building a business that scales with intention.This podcast will help you answer:How do I build a leadership team I can actually trust?How do I create systems that run without me?How do I scale to multiple locations without burning out?How do I increase profitability as I grow?How do I go from operator to CEO?Grab your copy of Multi-Unit Mastery at irfbook.com and follow Columbine Hospitality on LinkedIn.Voice Over, Mixing and Mastering Credits:L. Connor Voice - LConnorvoice@gmail.comLconnorvoice.comArtwork by Solstice Photography, Tucson, AZ. https://solsticephotography70.pixieset.com/

title: The Stellar Shifts Astrology Podcast
author: Elisheva | Stellar Shifts Astrology
description:
Personal astrology, plainly told — what's happening, what it means, what to do next.

Most astrology tells you who you are. Stellar Shifts tells you what to do next.

Join us for short weekly+ orientations on the moment you're in, along with deeper dives into the choices that matter.

title: No Referral Required
author: No Referral Required
description: Ever wonder what your doctor is actually thinking? No Referral Required is a weekly conversation hosted by two physicians and a non-doctor who's just as curious as you are. Together they demystify the medical world — how doctors are trained, how they make decisions, and what they wish their patients understood. Each week they're joined by a guest from a different specialty to share the human side of medicine, in plain language anyone can follow. No referral, and no medical degree, required.

title: Like A Diamond Podcast
author: Like A Diamond Podcast
description:
Trials that shape us into something rare-
Deep in the earth, under conditions no living thing could survive, something extraordinary happens. Carbon — ordinary carbon — is transformed into the hardest, most brilliant substance in nature.
We believe the same is true of the human spirit. The pressures, heartbreaks, and impossible seasons we face aren't accidents. They are the forge. 
Like a Diamond Podcast features real stories of struggle, trials, and the breathtaking beauty that emerges on the other side. Because diamonds aren't formed in comfort — and neither are we.

title: Entertainment Media Path - EMPath
author: Carolyn DeClue, Beau DeClue
description: EMPath explores the systems behind creative work—where ideas meet strategy, and careers are built with intention. Each episode breaks down the tools, workflows, and decisions that move your work forward across film, design, writing, music, and media.

title: Pipe Dojo
author: Pipe Dojo
description: Philosophical thoughts and reflections on life, society and books from a fellow piper!

title: Get the Girlies Moving!
author: Kaitlyn and Abigail
description: Get the Girlies Moving is a podcast about women's health, fitness, and wellness hosted by Kaitlyn and Abigail. Each week, we cut through the doom scroll noise of Instagram at 2am and break down what science actually says, backed by real research. We share our own experiences, bring on guests you can relate to, and have plenty of laughs along the way. This isn't just a podcast to listen to, it's a space to feel empowered, learn something new, and become the best version of you. New episodes every Wednesday.

title: The Girlies Report Podcast ❤️
author: Thegirliereport
description:
Just a girl with a microphone and way too many opinions. 🎙️

Winnipeg life, beauty, dating, motherhood, reality TV, and whatever else is living rent free in my head. 😊

Have a story to share? Need Advice? Want to submit some tea, a confession, a dating disaster. or a topic you'd like me to talk about? ✍️

Reach out - anonymous or not. My inbox is always open -thegirliesreport@gmail.com 📩

title: Not Just Because - Biology
author: Mutual Pledge Incorporated
description: Life science for kids around 7 to 10, covering bodies, cells, ecosystems, and living systems in clear, concrete terms without talking down.

title: Real TALK Gospel With Jon
author: Jonathan Almand
description: Talking about Christ. Giving the Gospel Truth.

title: THE ULTRAMAG 7
author: ULTRAMAG7
description: I don’t know about you, but I can’t stand cookie-cutter interviews with Hip-Hop artists. You know the drill...Who are your Top 3 Favorite Emcees? Who are your Top 5 Favorite Producers? What’s your Favorite Track that you’ve ever made? Yeah, yeah… all fine questions, but, that's not my lane. Me, I like to throw curveballs. I like to hit them with the unexpected and make them think. That’s what THE ULTRAMAG 7 is all about. 7 random questions, straight from me, ULTRAMAG7, to my friends, Hip-Hop heroes, and fellow movers and shakers in the culture.

title: Three Knights and a Page
author: Andrew, Ryan, Casey
description:
Hello and welcome to Three Knights and a Page, your fantasy study guide,  a comedic literary review podcast, hosted by three individuals who are decidedly not knights and who (ideally) cover more than one page. We will be your guide, your shepherd through the endless miasma that is fantasy and fantasy reading. Join us every other Monday as we cover a different fantasy book, chapter by chapter, and hope we don't make absolute fools of ourselves along the way. 
So whether you're a fantasy expert, or you're new to the genre, Ryan, Casey and Andrew invite you to answer the call! Your destiny awaits!

title: The Bleeding Edge
author: BloodStream Media
description:
A condition long observed. A burden long carried. A conversation long overdue.
Glanzmann thrombasthenia is often described as a rare platelet disorder defined by bleeding symptoms. But for patients, caregivers, and clinicians, the lived reality extends far beyond that definition.
This five-part podcast series features four immersive episodes and a culminating roundtable conversation that together examine what it means to live with GT in a system that has long recognized the condition, yet continues to grapple with its full impact on everyday life.
Through intimate conversations with patients, caregivers, and clinical experts, the series explores the gap between what is known about GT and what is experienced in real life. From daily symptom burden and care coordination challenges to the ongoing effort required to access appropriate treatment, The Bleeding Edge examines the visible and invisible weight of GT, and what it demands from those who live with it along with those who care for them.
Produced by BloodStream Media. Made possible by Hemab Therapeutics.

title: Shift Happens Podcast
author: Newport Healthcare
description: A podcast by Newport Healthcare, Shift Happens features stories of hope and healing from youth, young adults, and families; trends in mental healthcare; and best practices for lifelong recovery. This podcast, produced by an industry-leading behavioral health company, is for anyone who is struggling with or supporting a young person with their mental health.

title: Puzzle The Listeners
author: Duncan
description: I create audio-experiences that are not your typical podcast conversation.

title: The FedEx Ground Safety Tracker
author: PNC2931
description:
The FedEx Ground Safety Tracker 

Every single day, thousands of FedEx Ground trucks navigate our nation’s roads and highways under intense delivery pressure. But when the pressure to deliver results in a devastating commercial vehicle accident, victims are often left facing catastrophic injuries, mounting medical bills, and an aggressive corporate defense machine.

FedEx Watch: The Injury Alliance is the definitive national watch platform and safety tracker dedicated to holding commercial carrier giants accountable. Each episode breaks down real-time accident investigations, uncovers corporate safety failures, and decodes the complex world of commercial trucking litigation.

But we don't just report on the wreckage—we map out your route to justice.

Navigating a commercial truck claim requires a highly specialized legal advocate. This podcast serves as your ultimate guide to finding the nation's top personal injury lawyers who specialize in commercial fleet litigation. We provide victims and their families with the exact blueprint needed to cut through the corporate noise, protect their rights, and secure the compensation they deserve.

Don't face the corporate fleet alone. Tune in, stay informed, and find the right advocate for your recovery.

title: Ways of Peace
author: Initiatives of Peace
description: Is peace possible? In yourself? In your family? At work? In the world? Zack Muetterties (Founder of Initiatives of Peace) and Bek Hoover (Wave Media) discuss some of the key ways peace has emerged in relationships across the globe. This series is shared in the hope that others might be encouraged to take steps towards a more beautiful way and world, no matter their situation or background.

title: Great Measure
author: Keolani Young
description:
There's a version of your work that is fully expressed, widely recognized, and built to last. Great Measure is the podcast for visionary leaders, creators, and business owners who are building a life that reflects the true measure of their potential.

Hosted by Business Consultant Keolani Young, each episode explores the performance principles, leadership mechanics, esoteric systems, and cognitive frameworks behind doing your best work. Expect honest conversations about execution, creative output, decision-making, and how to turn your natural gifts into your most prolific asset.

title: Episode 1 - Introduction to Luxury Transportation Academy
author: Dr. Morris Parker
description:
Welcome to Episode 1 of the Luxury Transportation Podcast by Luxury Transportation Academy.

In this episode, Dr. Morris Parker shares his journey of building a successful luxury transportation company, the mindset required to grow a profitable chauffeur business, and why systems, technology, customer service, and mentorship are essential for long-term success.

Whether you're starting a black car, limousine, or executive transportation business—or looking to scale an existing operation—this podcast delivers practical insights and proven strategies from real industry experience.

🎙️ Subscribe for more episodes covering luxury transportation, chauffeur excellence, business growth, marketing, operations, and entrepreneurship.

Learn more about the Luxury Transportation Academy and take your transportation business to the next level.

title: The Occultverse Podcast
author: Vikkas Rana
description:
I am Vikkas Rana, and this podcast is a walk through the scriptures and sacred systems that many of us are told to choose between — as if the Bhagavad Gita and the Tantra Raj Tantram, or the Gurbani and the Das Mahavidya, couldn't possibly speak to the same seeker. I've spent my life proving otherwise.

Each episode draws a lesson from one of these traditions — the Rudrayamalam Tantra's teachings on hidden power, Krishna's counsel to Arjuna in the Gita, the ten wisdoms of the Das Mahavidya, or the verses of Sri Japji Sahib and Sri Guru Granth Sahib — and unpacks what they actually ask of a person trying to live rightly in a difficult world. Not academic commentary. Not borrowed spirituality. A practitioner's reading, tested against real questions from real people, across traditions that rarely get to sit in the same room.

title: Conversations That Make a Difference with Teresa Velardi
author: Teresa Velardi
description:
Welcome to Conversations That Make a Difference with Teresa Velardi | Podcast — inspiring conversations that uplift, heal, educate, and empower.

Hosted by author, speaker, publisher, and storyteller Teresa Velardi, this podcast features meaningful conversations with bestselling authors, thought leaders, wellness experts, spiritual teachers, entrepreneurs, healers, advocates, survivors, and changemakers who are making a positive impact in the world.

Each episode explores topics including personal growth, healing, spirituality, wellness, resilience, mindset, conscious living, emotional well-being, self-discovery, purpose, empowerment, relationships, transformation, overcoming adversity, and the power of the human spirit.

Through authentic storytelling, wisdom-filled conversations, and transformational insights, Teresa creates a space where stories inspire hope, healing, courage, and meaningful change. Whether you are seeking encouragement, spiritual growth, wellness inspiration, deeper connection, or practical tools for navigating life’s challenges, this podcast offers heartfelt conversations designed to nourish the soul and expand possibility.

Featuring inspiring human stories, author interviews, transformational teachings, wellness conversations, and powerful messages of hope — because every story has the power to make a difference.

title: Reformation Presbyterian Church Sermons
author: RPC Pike Road, AL
description: Welcome to the sermon podcast of Reformation Presbyterian Church in Pike Road, Alabama. Our desire is to proclaim the whole counsel of God through faithful, Christ-centered exposition of the Scriptures. New sermons are added weekly following each Lord's Day service.

title: Made at Michigan: Engineering Alumni Stories
author: University of Michigan Engineering
description:
A University of Michigan Engineering degree opens doors. Our alumni launch startups, lead major companies, design life-saving technologies and make an impact across industries. They are innovators, leaders and problem-solvers. Their journeys show the power of engineering to change lives, including their own. 


Listen to audio versions of short stories that highlight a few of Michigan Engineering’s 100,000+ alumni by subscribing to Made at Michigan: Engineering Alumni Stories, available on Spotify, Apple Podcasts and YouTube. 


Or you can read them here: https://news.engin.umich.edu/alumni-impact/


*These stories were written by Michigan Engineering staff. The audio was generated using AI.



Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: Too Luckies The Podcast Dyin, Cryin and Lyin
author: Too Luckies The Podcast
description:
Too Luckies, talking about what we notice in Little House on the Prairie now that we are adults. We feel like each of their episodes has some element of Dyin, Cryin and Lyin, with at least 2 out of 3 in each. 
Come explore how many each episode has as we go through the seasons. Hopefully you’ll get a laugh.

title: Truth Beyond
author: Josh and Frank
description: Exploring the World of UFOs and Paranormal

title: Soporific
author: Dr Tench Media
description: A sleep companion for the restless mind. Ten episodes. A breathing routine. Poetry read aloud in the quiet hours. Hosted by The Dreamcatcher.

title: All Bassists Are Cool
author: Zach Pyke
description: Comedian and Professional bassist Zach Pyke has a hypothesis, and with the help  of his non-biased comedian guest hosts he'll go in depth interviewing bassists of all stripes to finally prove to science and the world that it is indeed a fact that: "All Bassists are Cool."

title: Word Of Righteousness by EGFMUSA
author: EGFMUSA
description:
The Eternal Glorious Fountain Ministry is positioned in the body of Christ to supply resources for the growth and maturity of believers; so the body can gradually and ultimately inherit the image and likeness of God.
The template for getting this done is in the person of our Lord Jesus Christ, who is the pattern Son that God desires all believers to be conformed to.

title: Anthony Colón, RN | Wellness, Weight Loss & Hormone Optimization
author: Anthony
description: Join Anthony Colón, RN, a registered nurse with more than 25 years of healthcare experience, for evidence-based conversations about GLP-1 weight loss medications, testosterone replacement therapy (TRT), peptides, hormone optimization, longevity, men’s health, women’s health, and telehealth. Each episode helps you separate facts from hype so you can make informed decisions about your health.

title: Craftory | PragerU Kids
author: PragerU Kids
description:
Craftory — A Creative Way for Kids to Learn About America!

Looking for a fun, educational, and hands-on podcast for your child? Craftory is the perfect show for curious kids in Kindergarten through 3rd grade who love learning and creating. Each episode of Craftory brings American history to life through engaging stories and easy DIY crafts that kids can do at home or in the classroom.

Your child will love discovering:

🔹 The meaning behind American holidays like Independence Day, Presidents' Day, and Thanksgiving

🔹 Inspiring stories about U.S. history, values, and traditions

🔹 Fun, family-friendly craft projects that make learning hands-on and exciting

This podcast is designed to:

✔️ Spark creativity and a love for America in kids

✔️ Support parents and educators with age-appropriate educational content

✔️ Make learning about history, patriotism, and holidays meaningful—and fun!

Whether you’re at home, on the go, or adding to your homeschool routine, Craftory is the perfect companion for families who want to combine educational stories, DIY fun, and America for kids all in one place.

🎯 Follow PragerU Kids for more educational fun!

📘 Facebook: facebook.com/PragerUKids

📸 Instagram: instagram.com/pragerukids

📌 Pinterest: pinterest.com/pragerukids

▶️ YouTube: youtube.com/PragerUKids

🌐 Website: pragerukids.com

✨ Craftory — Where learning meets American values. Subscribe now and help your child discover the heroes, holidays, and history that make our country special—one craft at a time!

title: Too Far Gone
author: Justin McLane
description: Too Far Gone is a podcast for people who know that feeling. Not the polished testimony version of it. The real one. The kind where you are not sure you are coming back, where the people closest to you have already written you off, and where the idea of redemption sounds like something that happens to other people.

title: Out of Bounds with Bo Bounds
author: Out of Bounds Sports
description: The Out of Bounds Show is where sports, culture, and the Southern lifestyle collide. Hosted by Bo Bounds, this high-energy daily show delivers SEC football, NFL headlines, and Mississippi insight with a sharp edge and a storyteller’s touch. But this isn’t just another sports show.  From big-time guests and insider interviews to conversations on bourbon, food, travel, and the finer things in life, Out of Bounds brings a premium, entertaining experience to every episode. This show is built for listeners who want more than just scores.

title: Press play:teen voice
author: Press Play: Teen Voices
description: We talk about school stress, exam managing data and screen time and gives every solution to every challenge as a teenager

title: Locked On NFL Big Board with Michelle Magdziuk - Daily NFL Ranking Podcast
author: Locked On Podcast Network, Michelle Magdziuk
description: The Locked On NFL 6 Big Board Podcast is your daily NFL rankings podcast giving die-hard fans an in-depth look at the National Football League's biggest stories, players and moments. Hosted by Michelle Magdziuk, a NFL researcher for ESPN, the Locked On NFL NFL Big Board podcast will get you your daily NFL fix with expert analysis, rumors and opinions, ranked. From draft boards, coaches and power rankings to teams ready to spring into or fall out of competition. The hottest takes with the best research to back it all up. The Locked On NFL Big Board is part of the Locked On Podcast Network. Your Team. Every Day.

title: The Sensing Self Meditations
author: Kaya Allison Paradis
description: Guided meditations for deep feelers, overthinkers, and sensitive souls.

title: The moralssoldseparately’s Podcast
author: moralssoldseparately
description:
Morals Sold Separately is a podcast built on what most people try to hide. Each week, anonymous guests join our masked host to reveal the real stories behind fraud, deception, betrayal, and the kind of moral failure that never makes it to court — but deserves to be heard.

From shady business deals to personal betrayals, romantic scams to corporate coverups, this is the podcast for anyone who’s ever trusted the wrong person… and lived to tell the tale.

Guests wear masks. Voices are altered. The stories? 100% real.

If you’ve ever looked back and thought, “That was wrong — and they got away with it,” this show is for you.


Behind the mask, we tell the truth.

title: Anxiously Ever After
author: Jennifer Ephgrave
description:
Honest, sweary, slightly chaotic conversations about anxiety, neurodivergence, menopause, and the general mess of being human. Hosted by Jennie—52, waiting for ADHD and autism assessments, living in a rented room in an HMO, door locked, figuring it out in real time. No toxic positivity. No "have you tried yoga?" Just real talk from someone who gets it. New episodes weekly. Grab a cuppa. You'll probably forget to drink it. That's fine. 💚
You can support the podcast here 👉 https://buymeacoffee.com/jen1409m

title: Sox & Friends
author: Thomas Alessio
description:
Your weekly source for Chicago Whitesox updates, content and breaking news. Also, bringing the voice of the fan to the podcast with episodes recorded and shot during Whitesox tailgates. Connecting the fans directly with other fans throughout the world. 
Follow thomas_alessio on Threads for direct day to day updates and @SoxNFriends on Instagram

title: GA4-240202501-AA2-EV02. Audio.
author: dayanna
description: audio

title: Growing Brands with Melia Moore
author: Melia Moore
description:
The marketing podcast for founders trying to grow without the noise.

Most marketing advice is built for big budgets and big teams, then repackaged until every condition that made it work gets stripped out. Growing Brands is for the founder doing real revenue, running the whole thing themselves, who's tired of tactics that assume a context they're not in.

Host Melia Moore spent her late twenties in the agency world before going independent in Austin. Each week she takes one marketing idea — conversion, email, retention, paid ads, metrics — and works through what actually applies when you're small, with the evidence to back it and none of the hype. Measured, opinionated, and clear. No vanity metrics, no noise.

title: The Menu Has Secrets
author: Green Oasis
description: The Menu Has Secrets is a short podcast about the hidden psychology and business strategy behind food choices.Each episode explores how menus, restaurants, supermarkets, delivery apps, packaging, prices, smells, portions, and cravings quietly influence what people buy and eat.This is not a cooking show. It is a podcast about food, psychology, marketing, and the small decisions hiding inside every menu.

title: The Ballot Brief
author: Chelsea Seckora
description: The Ballot Brief is a podcast series that features conversations with candidates in Wisconsin's Democratic gubernatorial primary. Focusing on issues that matter to the business community, the series will explore workforce, economic development, education, energy, and other key topics ahead of the August primary election.

title: The Embodied Witch
author: Sabine Andree
description:
Have you ever felt like there's more to life than this? 
More than following the rules, or doing what you’re supposed to do. More than constantly doubting yourself, overthinking everything, and shrinking parts of who you are to fit in.
This podcast is for women who feel called to something deeper.
To me, the witch isn't a woman casting spells in the woods. She's the woman who trusts herself, reclaims her power, and remembers who she is. Together, we'll explore embodiment, spirituality, intuition, feminine power, and the sacred magic of living in alignment with who you really are.

title: When What If Happens
author: Jason Brown
description: When What If Happens explores the moments that change everything — real conversations with authors, entrepreneurs, and leaders about the decisions, detours, and what-ifs that shaped who they became. Hosted by Jason Brown.

title: LET GOD IN!
author: Becoming Precious
description: LET GOD IN! is a podcast dedicated to sharing powerful, uninterrupted stories of faith. Believers from all walks of life share their personal messages about experiencing grace, overcoming trials, and growing spiritually. Join us as we listen to everyday Christians and leaders share exactly how they have seen God move in their lives, providing encouragement for anyone looking to do the same.

title: The Scandalous Life of RJ
author: RJ Scandalous
description: One woman. Two countries. Endless adventures. From travel and motherhood to new beginnings and unexpected twists and turns, welcome to The Scandalous Life of RJ.

title: Holy Fire with Miguel and Alyese Carmona
author: Miguel Carmona
description: Join us as we talk about all aspects of life in family, marriage, parenting, ministry, and how God has done so much in our lives. It is our prayer that God will use our story to help those around us as we walk through this life together.

title: Art or Fart
author: Toxic Phreke and Fix It In Post Productions
description: Irreverent (or is it irrelevant) show where we review three seemingly different movies, but they do share a common thread. We review, make fun of, and decide which is art [dramatic pause] and which is a fart.

title: The A-Player Show
author: Ugis Balmaks
description:
The A-Player is the show for 8 and 9-figure founders tired of being the bottleneck in their own company.

Host Ugis Balmaks is a founder turned headhunter who's hunted true A-players for 100+ eCommerce and SaaS founders. The best people aren't applying. You have to hunt them.

Every week, get tactical on how to stop doing everyone's job and hire the people who move the needle.

title: Vedic Astrology Decoded
author: AstroVector Labs
description:
Vedic Astrology Decoded breaks down real Jyotish technique — not vague predictions. Each series takes one birth chart and reads it through three classical systems: Parashari Shadbala, KP sub-lord analysis, and Jaimini Char Dasha — so you see exactly how three different methods can look at the same placement and reach different insights.

Sidereal (Lahiri ayanamsa) throughout. If you've ever wanted an astrology show that shows its work instead of just declaring outcomes, this is it.

Educational content only — not a substitute for professional consultation. This show is AI-researched and AI-narrated.

title: Little Less Wrong
author: Little Less Wrong
description: What if the secret to moving forward isn't pretending you have all the answers, but admitting what you get wrong? On Little Less Wrong, Dikamsi and Ugo document their journey through life, career, and personal evolution. Each episode pairs raw personal stories with insights from the literature, design principles, and mental models they are studying in real-time. This isn’t a lecture from experts; it’s an open dialogue for anyone looking to optimize their mindset, learn from collective missteps, and make slightly better decisions every single day.

title: SciResMethods
author: Moonbow Tech
description:
Practical research methods for graduate students, researchers, and early-career academics. Each episode accompanies a SciResMethods framework PDF and provides actionable guidance on research planning, literature reviews, research design, research quality, research execution, thesis development, presentations, and academic communication. 
Download companion PDFs, research toolkits, and bundled resources from the Moonbow Tech, LLC storefront: https://moonbow.lemonsqueezy.com

title: Birding by Ear with the Master Birders Academy
author: Kayla Fisk
description:
Birding by ear can be one of the most important skills you can learn as a birder. Join me every week as we tackle the challenges of birding by ear learn how to decipher sounds and species-by-species, become a master of birding by ear.


I aim for this podcast to be a valuable resource for those who want to become a Master Birder and is hosted through the Master Birders Academy.

title: Cultural world
author: Amir
description:
Welcome to our self-help channel, a space dedicated to personal growth, motivation, and transformation. Here you will find content to strengthen your mindset, develop positive habits, overcome challenges, and build a more balanced and fulfilling life.

Our mission is to inspire you to believe in your potential and take small steps every day toward becoming the best version of yourself.

title: ESPECIAL BANDNEWS - O FUTURO EM RISCO
author: Band Minas
description: Enchentes costeiras, secas, calor extremo, queimadas, ondas de calor, enchentes de rios, tempestades de areia e poeira e tempestades tropicais. São várias as ameaças climáticas que se impõem não apenas aos adultos, mas sobretudo às crianças. Os impactos não são apenas físicos, mas psicológicos e sociais.

title: 'A Dog's Pace'
author: Laura Dobb
description:
A monthly with Slow Dog Movement founder, Laura Dobb and her guests to bring our latest musings on all things SLOW and interesting in the dog world. 
Listen to the full episodes of our newer podcasts by joining our PATREON page: patreon.com/slowdogmovement
This podcast contains Royalty Free music by Steve Oxen called 'Riviera Walk.' Copyright by Fesliyan Studios Inc. Royalty Free Music and Sound Effects Services

title: Impressionable Minds
author: Breeze Media
description:
Impressionable Minds is a culture, sports, comedy, and conversation podcast hosted by Pat The Designer and The Kid Wiz.
Every episode brings real conversations, big personality, honest opinions, and hilarious debates about the topics people are actually talking about. From NBA Free Agency, Chicago sports, the NFL, music, relationships, internet culture, viral stories, life, fatherhood, business, and everything in between, Pat and Wiz bring the kind of unfiltered conversations that feel like you’re sitting in the room with them.
The show also features fan-favorite segments like AITJ, where Pat and Wiz react to wild situations, debate who was right or wrong, and give their honest takes with humor, perspective, and zero fake outrage.
Whether you’re here for sports talk, culture conversations, comedy, hot debates, or just two real friends talking through life and the world around them, Impressionable Minds is the place for curious minds, strong opinions, and real conversation.
Hosted by Pat The Designer and The Kid Wiz.
Powered by Breeze Media.

title: UNHINGED with Dr. Michael Meneghini
author: Dr. Michael Meneghini
description:
Real conversations in healthcare and MedTech, said out loud.
This is a podcast where experienced operators speak honestly about healthcare, business, and leadership in a setting that feels human, not staged. This is NOT chaos for the sake of chaos “unhinged.” It’s controlled honesty from credible people.

title: One Shot Away
author: Gavin
description:
In the world of sports, greatness isn't given—it’s taken. A legendary buzzer-beater, a 9th-inning walk-off, a clinical top-corner free kick, or a championship-winning putt on the 18th green. Every iconic moment comes down to the exact same thing: being One Shot Away.
Join cousins Gavin and Joe every week as they break down the biggest stories, hottest takes, and deepest analyses across the sports landscape. Whether you are obsessed with soccer, golf, basketball, baseball, or football, we've got you covered with the perfect blend of analytical breakdowns and casual, unfiltered fan debate.

title: Sit, Breathe, Bow
author: Ian White Maher
description: Each week leading Buddhist teachers share life experiences and insights to help guide your meditation practice as well as your life off the cushion.

title: METROBOYZ
author: Ricckylf noblecee
description:
MetroBoyz is Detroit’s first gay male reality show, created to showcase bold personalities, real-life experiences, competition, talent, and authentic connections. The series follows a group of young gay men as they navigate friendships, challenges, drama, personal growth, and unforgettable moments while representing themselves and their city.

More than just a reality show, MetroBoyz is a platform for individuals to be seen, heard, and create opportunities for themselves. With a mix of entertainment, brotherhood, games, talent, competition, and real Detroit energy, the show delivers raw, exciting, and unfiltered content that keeps viewers engaged.

MetroBoyz is about creating history, building a community, and giving gay men a space to showcase who they are while bringing something fresh and unique to Detroit’s entertainment scene.

title: The Biz Files Podcast
author: With Madison Dinkelmann & De Gale
description: In-studio conversations with businesses & non-profits in North GA about success, setbacks, and the road ahead.

title: Rachel Lawson's The Magicians: Why Am I Always Juliet? podcast
author: Rachel Lawson
description:
What happens when a storm‑summoning necromancer and the immortal embodiment of Fate get forced into Shakespeare’s most romantic tragedy?

Absolute anarchy.

Why Am I Always Juliet? follows Blake — Death himself disguised as Queen Elizabeth’s weatherman — and Lucius Silver, a time‑lost Shakespearean actor who has played kings, gods, and legends… but never the one role he wants.

Instead, he’s Juliet.

Again.

Across three acts of escalating chaos, Blake and Lucius battle wardrobe disasters, magical misfires, dagger incidents, balcony collapses, and Shakespeare’s relentless enthusiasm. Rumors swirl that Romeo and Juliet are witches. The cast panics. The Queen expects brilliance. And Shakespeare keeps fainting.

Every rehearsal goes wrong.

Every scene becomes a supernatural accident.

Every attempt at romance ends in lightning, levitation, or attempted murder.

And somehow… the audience loves it.

A comedic fantasy of fate, friendship, and theatrical disaster, Why Am I Always Juliet? is a backstage romp through Elizabethan England where Death plays Romeo, Fate plays Juliet, and Shakespeare insists they’re “visionaries.”

The songs are coming.

The chaos is already here.

Hosted on Acast. See acast.com/privacy for more information.

title: The Sports Reset Canada
author: Digital Saffron
description: Canada’s fast daily sports update covering the biggest scores, stats, and news across the NHL, NFL, CFL, MLB, NBA, soccer, tennis, golf, F1, and more.  Built for casual fans who want to stay current without hours of commentary, hot takes, or fluff.  Available 365 days a year every morning. Produced with AI assistance and human oversight.

title: SanClub
author: sanclubgoinnet
description: SanClub - Các ý kiến đóng góp đều được ghi nhận để làm cơ sở cải thiện chất lượng dịch vụ trong tương lai. Song song với đó, hệ thống thường xuyên tối ưu hiệu suất nhằm duy trì tốc độ truy cập ổn định, giảm thiểu tình trạng gián đoạn. Đây là yếu tố góp phần nâng cao mức độ hài lòng của nhiều người trong quá trình trải nghiệm. WEB: https://sanclubgo.in.net/

title: Who Kares?
author: Todd Lingel
description: Helping kids and adults make better choices in life.

title: Shrug: A Leadership Podcast
author: MOLE Theory, LLC
description:
Shrug is a leadership podcast for high performers who are quietly depleting themselves by carrying things ( obligations, tasks, standards) that aren't actually theirs to carry. The signature question every episode circles back to: "Is my contribution creating health, or just preventing someone else from facing consequences?"
Disclaimers:
Not affiliated with the Atlas Shrugged estate or Ayn Rand Institute. Scripture is paraphrased. Not therapy or professional advice. MBTI/Enneagram framing are popular self-reflection frameworks; not validated diagnostic tools.

title: BizTalk with the Wichita Business Journal
author: BizTalk with the Wichita Business Journal
description: The Wichita Business Journal is the leading source of business news in the Wichita area. Every Friday we’ll bring you an in-depth interview with local business leaders that you need to know. Join Kirk Seminoff and his guests and they discuss topics that are vital to the Wichita business community.
